content: Why are we invite only? Is there a reason not to open it up?

It isn't really "invitation only", but rather "just request it", i.e. all invitations are accepted right away. When I created the Zulip, I wanted to have it so that anybody could request an invitation in the join form, but that did not seem to be an option. Since I didn't know if spam was/would be a problem with Zulip, I decided to put the "request invitation" text in GitHub's README. But it is probably fine opening it up too.

One nice thing I found about that system is that people sending the email usually introduce themselves -- I enjoy reading those. I also take the opportunity to ask if they are joining on behalf of an organization or on their own.

For introductions you could have a separate introduction channel that new users are added to by default. That may result in less introductions though. Maybe the welcome message could suggest writing an introduction?
As for having an invitation request form, you could open an issue to add the option.

FWIW Rust zulip is way bigger and popular, and they get issues with spam only occasionally. I am not sure if the number of motivated Rust haters is greater or small than the number of motivated Rust In Linux haters.

Sorry to bring this topic up again. I really think we should open registration to new users without additional email requests.

Another nice thing to do would be to allow to read messages without signing in, as main Rust zulip does.

Yerkebulan Tulibergenov said:

Sorry to bring this topic up again. I really think we should open registration to new users without additional email requests.

Another nice thing to do would be to allow to read messages without signing in, as main Rust zulip does.

It would make the community more open and easier to approach :+1:

One more data point: Asahi Lina (who is trying to write GPU driver for M1 and M2 in Rust) mistakenly thought that zulip is invite only https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/70657af9-90bb-ee9e-4877-df4b14c134a5@asahilina.net/

Hi, I'm here now! Yes, it was kind of confusing not knowing where the community is... The GitHub is active, but the mailing list is fairly quiet and I didn't know where I could ask questions or just talk about the project.
